metadata.description: Chinese vocabulary for time expressions, social relationships, and reasons

content.name: now
content.side_g: 现在 is a time word (时间词) placed before or after the subject: 现在几点？ or 你现在忙吗？ Often pairs with 了 to mark a change of state. Contrast with 目前 (mùqián, 'at present'), which is more formal.

content.name: quarter_hour
content.side_f: a quarter past three (three o'clock one quarter)
content.side_g: 刻 = 15 minutes in time-telling. Use 一刻 (1:15 past), 三刻 (45 past). Note tone sandhi: 一刻 → yí kè. 两点三刻 = 2:45. Not used for minutes outside quarter-hour contexts.

content.name: matter_affair
content.side_f: I have a lot of things (to do).
content.side_g: 事(儿) is countable in Beijing speech with erhua: 事儿. Common collocations: 有事 (be busy/have something going on), 没事 (no problem / nothing going on). 没事 also means 'it's fine / never mind' as a response.

content.name: today
content.side_g: 今天 is a time word; can appear sentence-initial or after subject: 今天我很忙 / 我今天很忙 — both natural. Unlike English, no preposition needed. Contrast: 今日 (jīnrì) is literary/written.

content.name: very
content.side_f: He's a very nice person. (lit. he person very good)
content.side_g: 很 is semantically bleached before monosyllabic adjectives; 我忙 sounds incomplete/contrastive, so 我很忙 is the neutral default. When truly emphasizing 'very', stress it or use 非常 (fēicháng).

content.name: busy
content.side_g: 忙 is a stative adjective — no 是 needed: 我很忙 (not 我是忙). A-not-A form 忙不忙 is a common yes/no question. Can take an object informally: 忙工作 (busy with work).

content.name: tomorrow
content.side_g: 明天 parallels 今天/昨天. To specify time of day, stack modifiers: 明天早上 / 明天晚上 / 明天下午. No preposition before it when used adverbially: 明天见 (see you tomorrow).

content.name: dinner_supper
content.side_f: I'm inviting you to dinner. (I invite you eat dinner)
content.side_g: 晚饭 pairs with the verb 吃: 吃晚饭. Parallel forms: 早饭 (breakfast), 午饭 (lunch). In formal or southern usage, 晚餐 (wǎncān) is more common. 请 + person + VP means 'invite/treat someone to VP'.

content.name: why
content.side_g: 为什么 is a question pronoun that stays in-situ before the verb phrase — no fronting needed. Colloquial variant: 干吗 (gànmá). Answer with 因为…: 因为我很忙。

content.name: for
content.side_g: 为 (wèi) is a preposition: 'for/for the sake of'. In 为什么 it means 'for what (reason)'. Distinguish from 为 (wéi) 'to be/act as'. Common fixed phrase: 为人民服务 (serve the people).

content.name: because
content.side_g: 因为 often pairs with 所以 (suǒyǐ, 'therefore') — unlike English, Chinese keeps BOTH: 因为A，所以B. Can stand alone as an answer to 为什么. Written/formal: 由于 (yóuyú).

content.name: also_too
content.side_e: tā hái yǒu hěn duō shì
content.side_f: He still has a lot of things (to do). / He also has...
content.side_g: 还 has two key senses: (1) 'still/yet' (他还在忙) and (2) 'also/additionally' (我还认识他). Contrast with 也 (yě, 'also, parallel comparison') — 还 adds more of the same type; 也 draws a parallel between subjects.

content.name: classmate
content.side_g: 同学 literally 'same-study'. Used as both a noun ('classmate') and a vocative/title by teachers addressing students: 同学们好 (hello students). Compare 朋友 (relationship-based) vs 同学 (context-based).

content.name: recognize_acquainted
content.side_a: to be acquainted with; to recognize
content.side_f: I don't know him. (I am not acquainted with him)
content.side_g: 认识 = be acquainted with (people/characters), implying first-hand familiarity. Distinguish from 知道 (zhīdào, 'know a fact'): 我知道他，但不认识他 = I know of him but haven't met him. 识 is toneless here: rènshi.

content.name: friend
content.side_e: tā shì wǒ de hǎo péngyou
content.side_g: 朋友 is unmarked for gender; 男朋友/女朋友 specifically mean boyfriend/girlfriend (romantic), so be careful. 好朋友 = close friend. 交朋友 (jiāo péngyou) = make friends. 友 is toneless in 朋友: péngyou.
